narrative.headline: 2022 Subaru Ascent: power train tops the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 53 complaints with NHTSA. The power train is the most reported area at 25%, followed by the brakes (21%). No deaths are reported. The forward collision avoidance system draws the fewest of these complaints (9%) but the most crashes (2) and injuries (3) of any area, so it is worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 13 reports (25%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries logged. Owners describe transmission slipping and lunging.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 11 reports (21%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries logged. Listen for squealing or grinding when braking.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 7 reports (13%), including 1 crash and 1 fire. Watch for hesitation when accelerating from a stop or while passing.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: 7 reports (13%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries logged. Owners mention windshield chipping early in the car's life.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Fewest of these complaints (5 reports, 9%) but the most serious by outcome: 2 crashes and 3 injuries. Test the collision-avoidance behavior carefully.
